Describe The Record (Notes)

BOSON [II] de Châtellerault, 

son of HUGUES [I] Vicomte de Châtellerault & his wife Gerberge --- (-after [1108]).  

"Hugo vicecomes de Castello Airaudi…et uxor sua Girberga" renounced rights in favour of Saint-Hilaire de Poitiers by charter dated to [1055], which refers to "filios enim quos habebant non permittebat Deus diu vivere" and "Bosone…eorum filio"[648].  Vicomte de Châtellerault.  "Boso vicecomes de Castello Ayraudi" confirmed the renunciation of rights by "pater meus Hugo vicecomes et mater mea Erberga" in favour of Saint-Hilaire de Poitiers, by charter dated to [1076][649].  "…Boso vicecomes de Castello Ayraudi…" affirmed the actions of "Amatus [archiepiscopi Burdagelensisi Legatique apostolici]" in the charter dated [1090][650].  An undated charter (dated to [1102]) records that “Boso vicecomes Castri Adraldi et Bartholomæus de Insula et Hugo de Sancta Maura” besieged “rupem...Mirmanda” and attacked Faye, and that subsequent donations were made to Noyers[651].  “Boso proconsul Castri Araldi” donated “viam...Busseriæ” to Noyers, with the support of “uxor proconsulis et duo filii eius Aimericus et Boso et filia eius”, by charter dated to [1108][652]. 

m ([1075] ELEONORE de Thouars, daughter of AIMERY [IV] Vicomte de Thouars & his first wife Orengarde de Mauléon (-after [1108]).  "Bossonis vicecomitis, Aimerici filius eius, Adenordis vicecomitissæ" witnessed a charter dated [1082] under which "Erchembaldus Grenulla" donated property to Saint-Jean d'Angély[653].  The primary source which confirms her parentage has not yet been identified.  "Boso vicecomes et uxor eius Adenors et filii eorum" donated property to St Cyprien by charter dated 1088 subscribed by "Bosonis vicecomitis et Aenoris uxoris eius et filiorum ipsorum Aimerici et Bosonis et sororis eorum Gisberge"[654].  "Willelmus Goscelinus monachus Sancti Cipriani" donated property to St Cyprien by charter dated [1088] subscribed by "Boso vicecomes, Ainors uxor, filii ipsorum Aimericus, Boso et Petrus"[655].  "Castri Araldus vicecomes…Boso…cum uxore Adenorde et filio Aimerico" donated property "allodium…in Brigisensi pago apud villam Culturas" to the abbey of Saint-Florent near Saumur by undated charter subscribed by "filii eius minoris Bosonis"[656].  “Boso proconsul Castri Araldi” donated “viam...Busseriæ” to Noyers, with the support of “uxor proconsulis et duo filii eius Aimericus et Boso et filia eius”, by charter dated to [1108][657]. 

Boson [II] & his wife had five children: 

1.         AIMERY [I] (-Abbaye de Noyers 7 Nov before 1144, bur Abbaye de Noyers).
2.         BOSON .
3.         PIERRE (-after 1135).
4.         ACFRED .  [1090].
5.         GISBERGE .
6.         daughter (-after [1108]).

sourceList.title: Foundation for Medieval Genealogy - BOSON [II] de Châtellerault, son of HUGUES [I] Vicomte de Châtellerault & his wife Gerberge --- (-after [1108]).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault於大約1036年出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰維安a特勒勞爾特時，他的父親Hugues de Chatellerault I29歲，母親Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld13歲。他於1075年與Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1092年4月在法國波特-哈倫斯維安a特勒勞爾特享年57歲，埋葬在法國維安。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Boson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: possibly a variant of   Balson   or   Bulson  .

variants.name: Balson
meanings: English (Dorset): possibly a variant of the habitational name Balsdon, which is most probably from Great Boulsdon in Newent (Gloucestershire), Balstone in Buckland Monachorum, or Bulstone in Branscombe (both Devon). Alternatively, it may be derived from a Middle English personal name derived from an unattested Old English personal name formed with beald ‘bold’ + stān ‘stone’). Compare Bolson and Balsam .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault was born about 1036, in Châtellerault, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, his father, Hugues de Chatellerault I, was 29 and his mother, Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld, was 13. He married Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon in 1075. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in April 1092, in Châtellerault, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France, at the age of 57, and was buried in Vienne, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault est né environ 1036, à Châtellerault, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Hugues de Chatellerault I, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld, avait 13 ans. Il a épousé Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon en 1075. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en avril 1092, à Châtellerault, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, France, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Vienne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault um 1036 in Châtellerault, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugues de Chatellerault I 29 und seine Mutter Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld 13. Er hat 1075 Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb im April 1092 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Châtellerault, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Frankreich und wurde in Vienne, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault è nato il circa 1036 nel Châtellerault, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, il suo padre, Hugues de Chatellerault I, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld, aveva 13 anni. Lui sposato Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon nel 1075. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel aprile 1092, nel Châtellerault, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Vienne, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Vicomte Boson II de Chatelleraultが約1036年にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインヴィエンチaテラーアウルトで生まれた時，父Hugues de Chatellerault Iは29歳，母Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauldは13歳であった。1075年にAliénor de Thouars de Mauleonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1092年4月にフランスポイトウ-チャーエンツヴィエンチaテラーアウルトで57歳で死去し，フランスヴィエンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault(이)가 대략 1036년 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 비니 cha텔레라트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugues de Chatellerault I(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld(은)는 13세였다. 그는 1075년 Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1092년 4월 프랑스 포이토우-처츠 비니 cha텔레라트에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 비니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault nasceu aproximadamente 1036, em Châtellerault, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Hugues de Chatellerault I, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld, tinha 13 anos. Ele casou-se com Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon em 1075. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em abril de 1092, em Châtellerault, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, França, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Vienne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault родился около 1036 г. в местности чaтеллираулт, виенн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, его отцу по имени Hugues de Chatellerault I было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld, 13. Он женился на женщине по имени Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon в 1075 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер апрель 1092 г. в местности чaтеллираулт, виенн, поитоу-чарентесь, Франция в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности виенн, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Vicomte Boson II de Chatellerault nació aproximadamente 1036, en Châtellerault, Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Hugues de Chatellerault I, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Gerberga de laTour Rochefoucauld, tenía 13 años. Se casó con Aliénor de Thouars de Mauleon en 1075. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en abril de 1092, en Châtellerault, Vienne, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Vienne, Francia.
